LAW OFFICES

WALTER & PISTOLE, a p.c.
WATERFALL TOWERS
2455 BENNETT VALLEY ROAD, SUITE 200■ 17 10 43 AM '91
SANTA ROSA, CALIFORNIA 95404
(707) 523-0732
FACSIMILE NO. (707) 523-0924

JEFFREY A. WALTER*
VALERIE PISTOLE*
*A Professional Corporation
OF COUNSEL
LEWIS N. UNGER
RICHARD G. RYPINSKI

NAPA OFFICE
1006 JEFFERSON STREET
P.O. BOX 5748
NAPA, CALIFORNIA 94559
(707) 255-1885

October 15, 1991

REPLY TO: ______ SANTA ROSA

Susan Bobrow
Fair Political Practice Commission
428 J Street, Ste. 800
Sacramento, CA 95814

Re: Timber Cove Water District
Our File: Conflict of Interest/Tabbed

Dear Ms. Bobrow:

My firm represents the Timber Cove County Water District. I have spoken to you on several occasions regarding a potential conflict of interest of two of its Board members, one of whom owns two properties in the District and the other of which (with his wife in combination) owns 24 properties in the District. As you recall, the boundaries of this district are virtually coterminous with a subdivision (plus four parcels comprising the Timber Cove Inn).

At the present time, the District is contemplating drilling exploratory water wells within the limits of the District. Eight sites have been identified, two of which are located on these, two Board members' properties. The purpose of drilling these wells is to provide more water for the District and, potentially, allow the District to serve all of the properties in the subdivision. Currently, of the 275 parcels which make up the District (more or less), only 80 have water. Of the 24 parcels in which one Board member has an interest, only one has water. Of the two parcels the other Board member owns, only one has water. The other two Board members (there are a total of four Board members) each have their one parcel already served with water.

Under Regulation 18702.3(a)(2) the decision to drill these eight well sites (as well as other decisions related thereto) probably places the two Board members whose properties do not currently have water service in a conflict of interest. That is, the purpose of these wells is to locate sites for permanent water wells. The water from which is hoped to serve all of the remaining "un-watered" parcels. However, you and I discussed the possibility that the "public generally" exception might apply, depending on whether or not these two Board members would be considered impacted substantially similarly as is a

Susan Bobrow
October 15, 1991
Page 2

significant segment of the population of the jurisdiction. In
this connection, I have the following information to report to
you concerning the number of parcels owned by the various people
who have property located within the District:

Number of Property Owners          Number of Parcels Owned

146                                1

17                                 2

1                                  3

3                                  4

2                                  6

1                                  7

1                                  9

1                                  11

1                                  16

1                                  24

     Would you please call me after you have reviewed this
information so that we could discuss the "public generally"
exception again?

                                   Sincerely yours,

                                   JEFFREY A. WALTER, a p.c.

                                   [signature]
                                   JEFFREY A. WALTER

JAW/lmj

sections.question_synthetic: Whether two Timber Cove Water District Board members have a conflict of interest under Regulation 18702.5(a)(2) when voting on drilling exploratory water wells, two of which are located on their properties, and whether the "public generally" exception applies given that the wells would benefit a substantial portion of the district population.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter from an attorney to FPPC, not an FPPC response. Document lacks formal Q/A structure. Contains property ownership data table. Written as informal inquiry requesting FPPC staff review and telephone discussion. No formal FPPC conclusion or advice is provided in this document.

embedding.qa_text: QUESTION: Whether two Timber Cove Water District Board members have a conflict of interest under Regulation 18702.5(a)(2) when voting on drilling exploratory water wells, two of which are located on their properties, and whether the "public generally" exception applies given that the wells would benefit a substantial portion of the district population.

embedding.summary: A law firm representing Timber Cove Water District seeks FPPC guidance on whether two Board members who own multiple properties in the district have a conflict of interest in voting on proposed exploratory water well drilling, particularly where two well sites are on their properties.
